name: superdev
description: 18-skill full-stack toolkit plus 54 bundled role prompts for monorepo builds, audits, migrations, QA, debugging, and frontend refactoring. v1.4.0–v1.6.0 add a Laravel backend option — laravel-enterprise-backend (Laravel 13 + PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB + database cache/sessions + SQS; Eloquent API Resources + hand-written TS contracts; #[Audit] attribute; Title-Case enums; Laravel Boost) and laravel-bref-deploy (AWS Lambda serverless via Bref 3.x), plus design-to-laravel (Claude Design → Laravel + Inertia 3 + React 19 monolith) — selectable through orchestrator backend- and frontend-stack gates. v1.7.0 makes completion ENFORCED, not advisory: a machine-checkable Definition-of-Done gate (done-gate Stop hook + COMPLETION_LEDGER.json + the superdev-done skill) blocks any premature 'done'; the wave gate blocks lint errors + new suppressions and is stack-aware (TS tsc/eslint + Laravel Pint/PHPStan); and the self-learning loop actually fires. v1.8.0 adds production-readiness-audit — an iterative loop that scores every module 1–10 across five passes (completeness/wiring, data+seed, tenancy-isolation, exhaustive Playwright over every route/form/data-shape FE+BE, code-quality/security with zero suppressions) and loops fix→re-audit until every module is 9+, requiring real seed data + an initial owner/admin user and a tenancy-leak test.

interface.shortDescription: Full-stack build, audit, QA, migration, and refactoring skills.
interface.longDescription: Superdev brings Codex a skill-first full-stack toolkit for PRD-to-product builds, design-to-Next.js conversion, NestJS backends, prototype migration, QA, security review, debugging, product-completeness audits, frontend modularization, and atomic refactoring. The bundled Claude subagent files are kept as reusable role prompts; Codex runs the workflows through skills and explicit delegation.
defaultPrompt: Use Superdev to audit this product before ship.
Use Superdev to productionize this prototype.
Use Superdev to build from my PRD and design.
